title: Context integration, diff view, and polish
analysis:
Task 143 is the final integration layer for Claude session support in the Reify GUI. The codebase already has: (1) claudeStore with messages, sessionStatus, sendMessage(text, context)/handleOutboundMessage — context is currently Record<string,unknown>, (2) ChatPanel with input area (textarea + send/abort) and message rendering (For loop over messages, user messages and MessageGroup for assistant), (3) ConstraintPanel with expandable rows, onConstraintSelect callback, sorted by status, (4) StatusBar showing eval status/triangles/constraint counts in a section+divider pattern, (5) selectionStore with selectedEntity/hoveredEntity, (6) engineStore with constraints/values/evalStatus, (7) sidecar types with SendMessage.context defining selected_entity/diagnostics/constraints fields.

The work breaks into 7 sub-features: (A) Context injection UI — ContextPicker dropdown + ContextChip badges + wiring context into sendMessage, (B) Right-click 'Ask Claude' menus on ConstraintPanel rows, (C) Automatic context from selectionStore in every message, (D) Inline diff view for reify_update_source tool results, (E) Status bar Claude indicator showing sessionStatus, (F) Error classification and system messages in chat timeline, (G) First-run experience for auth errors.

Key design: system messages (errors, info) are modeled as a third role in the ChatMessage union (role='system') so they appear inline in the timeline at the correct position. Context is structured via a MessageContext type matching the sidecar's SendMessage.context shape. Diff is computed via a simple line-based LCS algorithm (no external dep). Error classification is a pure utility function. The ToolCallCard already detects reify_update_source and shows 'View diff' — we extend its expanded state to render a DiffView component.

prerequisites.description: Create gui/src/utils/ directory with diff.ts and errorClassifier.ts stub files

Extend claudeStore types: add SystemMessage type (role='system', id, errorType, text), update ChatMessage union to include SystemMessage, add MessageContext interface (selectedEntity?, diagnostics?, constraints?, currentFile?, attachedContexts?), add addSystemMessage() action to store. Update sendMessage to accept MessageContext parameter instead of Record<string,unknown>

steps.description: Write failing tests for errorClassifier utility in gui/src/__tests__/errorClassifier.test.ts: classifyError(message) returns { type, userMessage } for: (1) auth errors matching /auth|unauthorized|401/ → type='auth', userMessage='Authentication required. Run `claude login` in your terminal.'; (2) rate limit errors matching /rate.?limit|429/ → type='rate-limit', userMessage='Rate limited. Please wait and try again.'; (3) network errors matching /network|connect|ECONNREFUSED|fetch/ → type='network', userMessage='Connection failed. Check your network.'; (4) sidecar crash errors matching /disconnect|crash|exit|spawn/ → type='sidecar', userMessage='Claude session disconnected. Click to restart.'; (5) unknown errors → type='unknown', userMessage passes through the original message

Implement gui/src/utils/errorClassifier.ts: export interface ClassifiedError { type: 'auth'|'rate-limit'|'network'|'sidecar'|'unknown'; userMessage: string }. export function classifyError(message: string): ClassifiedError — pattern-match against ordered regex rules, return first match or unknown fallback

Write failing tests for diff utility in gui/src/__tests__/diff.test.ts: computeUnifiedDiff(before, after) returns DiffLine[] with { type: 'add'|'remove'|'context', content: string }. Test cases: (1) identical strings → all context lines; (2) pure additions (empty before, non-empty after) → all add lines; (3) pure deletions (non-empty before, empty after) → all remove lines; (4) single line change → one remove + one add; (5) mixed changes with context lines around them; (6) both empty → empty array; (7) multiline with insertions in the middle

Implement gui/src/utils/diff.ts: export interface DiffLine { type: 'add'|'remove'|'context'; content: string }. export function computeUnifiedDiff(before: string, after: string): DiffLine[] — split by newlines, compute LCS via dynamic programming, generate unified diff output with 3 lines of context around changes. Keep implementation simple (~60 lines), no external deps

Write failing tests for claudeStore extensions in gui/src/__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts — new describe block 'system messages': (1) addSystemMessage adds a message with role='system' to messages array; (2) system message has errorType and text fields; (3) system messages appear in correct position in timeline (after the last message); (4) error OutboundMessage with auth-pattern text adds a classified system message automatically; (5) error OutboundMessage with rate-limit pattern adds rate-limit system message; (6) first error that is auth-type includes first-run instructions in text

Extend gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts: add SystemMessage type, update ChatMessage union, add addSystemMessage() action, modify error handler to classify errors via classifyError() and add system messages. Add hasReceivedResponse flag to detect first-run auth failures. Update stores/index.ts exports to include new types

Write failing tests for SystemMessage component in gui/src/__tests__/systemMessage.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='system-message'; (2) displays message text; (3) auth errors have data-error-type='auth' and show 'claude login' text; (4) rate-limit errors have data-error-type='rate-limit'; (5) network errors have data-error-type='network'; (6) sidecar errors have data-error-type='sidecar' and show 'restart' text; (7) unknown errors display the raw message

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/SystemMessage.tsx + SystemMessage.module.css: Component<{errorType: string, text: string}>. Styled as centered system notification with warning icon, error-type-specific coloring via data-error-type attribute. Auth type shows code-formatted 'claude login' instruction. Export from chat/index.ts

Write failing tests for ContextChip component in gui/src/__tests__/contextChip.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='context-chip'; (2) displays label text prop; (3) shows remove button with data-testid='chip-remove'; (4) clicking remove calls onRemove callback; (5) different context types set data-context-type attribute ('selection', 'diagnostics', 'constraints', 'file'); (6) chip is keyboard-accessible (Enter on remove button triggers callback)

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/ContextChip.tsx + ContextChip.module.css: Component<{label: string, type: string, onRemove: () => void}>. Small pill/badge with label text, × dismiss button, colored by context type. Export from chat/index.ts

Write failing tests for ContextPicker component in gui/src/__tests__/contextPicker.test.tsx: (1) renders [+ context] button with data-testid='context-picker-btn'; (2) clicking button opens dropdown with data-testid='context-picker-dropdown'; (3) dropdown has 4 options: 'Current selection', 'Active diagnostics', 'Violated constraints', 'Current file'; (4) clicking an option calls onAttach(type) with the context type string; (5) dropdown closes after selecting an option; (6) dropdown closes on clicking outside; (7) options can be disabled when not available (e.g., no selection → 'Current selection' disabled)

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/ContextPicker.tsx + ContextPicker.module.css: Component<{onAttach: (type: ContextType) => void, hasSelection: boolean, hasDiagnostics: boolean, hasViolatedConstraints: boolean, hasActiveFile: boolean}>. Button opens positioned dropdown, options disabled based on availability props. Click-outside handler via document.addEventListener. Export from chat/index.ts

Write failing tests for DiffView component in gui/src/__tests__/diffView.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='diff-view'; (2) addition lines have data-diff='add' and green styling attribute; (3) deletion lines have data-diff='remove' and red styling attribute; (4) context lines have data-diff='context'; (5) starts expanded by default; (6) clicking header collapses the diff content; (7) clicking again re-expands; (8) when before===after shows 'No changes' text; (9) line numbers are displayed

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/DiffView.tsx + DiffView.module.css: Component<{before: string, after: string}>. Uses computeUnifiedDiff() to compute diff lines. Collapsible container (createSignal(true) for expanded). Each line rendered with data-diff attribute for type-based CSS (green background for add, red for remove, neutral for context). Monospace font, line numbers. Export from chat/index.ts

Write failing tests for ToolCallCard diff integration in gui/src/__tests__/ToolCallCard.test.tsx — new describe block 'diff view for source updates': (1) when toolName='reify_update_source', status='complete', and result has source content, expanding shows DiffView (data-testid='diff-view'); (2) DiffView receives before from toolInput and after from result; (3) for non-update tools, expanded details still show JSON (no diff view)

Modify gui/src/panels/chat/ToolCallCard.tsx: in the expanded details section, when toolCall.toolName === 'reify_update_source' && toolCall.status === 'complete', render DiffView instead of raw JSON pre block. Extract before string from toolCall.toolInput.content (or toolCall.toolInput.source) and after string from toolCall.result. Fall back to JSON display if result shape is unexpected

Write failing tests for ChatPanel context features in gui/src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx — new describe block 'context integration': (1) renders context picker button (data-testid='context-picker-btn') in input area; (2) renders context chips area (data-testid='context-chips') when items are attached; (3) attaching 'selection' context shows a ContextChip with selected entity label; (4) removing a chip removes it from display; (5) sending a message includes attached contexts in the sendMessage call args; (6) after sending, attached contexts are cleared; (7) when selectedEntity is provided, user messages show '[Context: X selected]' label (data-testid='auto-context-label'); (8) system messages in store.state.messages render as SystemMessage components (data-testid='system-message')

Modify gui/src/panels/ChatPanel.tsx: (1) add ChatPanelProps for selectedEntity, engineConstraints, engineValues, activeFile, diagnostics; (2) add createSignal<AttachedContext[]>([]) for context items; (3) render ContextPicker in inputArea with availability booleans derived from props; (4) render ContextChip list above input when items attached; (5) on handleSend, build MessageContext from attached items + automatic selectedEntity, pass to store.sendMessage, clear attached; (6) in message list For loop, handle role='system' by rendering SystemMessage; (7) show auto-context label '[Context: X selected]' on user messages when selectedEntity exists. Update ChatPanel.module.css with new styles for chips area and auto-context label

Write failing tests for ConstraintPanel 'Ask Claude' context menu in gui/src/__tests__/ConstraintPanel.test.tsx — new describe block 'Ask Claude context menu': (1) right-clicking (contextmenu event) a constraint row shows a menu with data-testid='constraint-context-menu'; (2) menu contains 'Ask Claude about this constraint' option; (3) clicking the option calls onAskClaude prop with constraint context string containing expression, status, and parameter values; (4) menu closes after clicking the option; (5) menu closes on subsequent click-outside; (6) onAskClaude is optional — omitting it means no context menu appears on right-click

Modify gui/src/panels/ConstraintPanel.tsx: add optional onAskClaude?: (context: string) => void prop. Add contextmenu event handler on constraint rows (only when onAskClaude is provided) that shows a positioned absolute div with the menu option. Build context string: 'Constraint: {expression}\nStatus: {status}\nParameters: {name}={value}, ...' from constraint data + resolved values. Click-outside handler to dismiss. Update ConstraintPanel.module.css with menu styles

Write failing tests for StatusBar Claude indicator in gui/src/__tests__/StatusBar.test.tsx — new describe block 'Claude status indicator': (1) when claudeStatus prop provided, renders section with data-testid='claude-status'; (2) shows 'Claude: idle' when claudeStatus='idle'; (3) shows 'Claude: thinking...' when claudeStatus='thinking'; (4) shows 'Claude: calling tool...' when claudeStatus='tool-calling'; (5) shows 'Claude: responding...' when claudeStatus='responding'; (6) clicking the indicator calls onToggleChat callback; (7) without claudeStatus prop, no claude-status section rendered (backwards compatible)

Modify gui/src/panels/StatusBar.tsx: add optional claudeStatus?: SessionStatus and onToggleChat?: () => void props. When claudeStatus is provided, render a new section with divider following the existing pattern: <span class={styles.divider}/> <span class={styles.section}> with label 'Claude:' and status text. Use data-claude-status attribute for per-state CSS styling (idle=muted, thinking=accent pulsing, tool-calling=accent, responding=accent, error=red). Make clickable with onClick={onToggleChat}. Update StatusBar.module.css

Write failing integration test in gui/src/__tests__/contextIntegration.test.tsx: (1) ChatPanel with engineStore constraints containing violated items → ContextPicker 'Violated constraints' option is enabled; (2) attaching 'Violated constraints' context and sending message → onSend receives constraints array in context; (3) ConstraintPanel onAskClaude callback with constraint context string → can be used to pre-populate ChatPanel input; (4) StatusBar with claudeStatus='thinking' → displays correct text; (5) error outbound message → system message appears in chat messages

Verify all sub-components integrate correctly. Fix any type mismatches between props. Ensure chat/index.ts exports new components (ContextPicker, ContextChip, DiffView, SystemMessage). Ensure panels/index.ts is unchanged (ChatPanel already exported)

Write failing test for App.tsx wiring in gui/src/__tests__/contextIntegration.test.tsx (additional cases): (1) verify ChatPanel receives selectionStore.selectedEntity as selectedEntity prop; (2) verify ConstraintPanel receives onAskClaude handler; (3) verify StatusBar receives claudeStatus from claudeStore.state.sessionStatus; (4) verify StatusBar onToggleChat toggles a chatOpen signal that controls ChatPanel visibility

Wire everything in gui/src/App.tsx: (1) pass selectedEntity={selectionStore.state.selectedEntity}, engineConstraints={engineStore.state.constraints}, engineValues={engineStore.state.values}, activeFile={editorStore.state.activeFile} to ChatPanel; (2) add onAskClaude handler to ConstraintPanel that builds context, sets ChatPanel input text, and opens chat panel if closed; (3) pass claudeStatus={claudeStore.state.sessionStatus} and onToggleChat to StatusBar; (4) add chatOpen signal to control ChatPanel visibility, toggled by StatusBar click. Use the existing useChatPanelPersistence hook for open/closed state

design_decisions.decision: Use a simple line-based LCS diff algorithm in utils/diff.ts instead of an external diff library
design_decisions.rationale: The project avoids unnecessary dependencies (hand-written FFI, custom JSON-RPC dispatcher). The diff needs are simple: compare two source strings line-by-line. A basic LCS algorithm is ~60 lines and sufficient for Reify source files which are typically small.

design_decisions.decision: Model system messages as a third role in ChatMessage union rather than a separate array
design_decisions.rationale: System messages (errors, info) need to appear inline in the chat timeline at the correct position relative to user/assistant messages. Adding them to the existing messages array with role='system' preserves ordering naturally and reuses the existing For-each rendering loop in ChatPanel.

design_decisions.decision: Context menu on ConstraintPanel uses a custom positioned div rather than browser native context menu
design_decisions.rationale: Tauri/webview context menus are inconsistent across platforms. A custom div positioned at click coordinates gives full control over styling and behavior, matching the project's existing pattern of custom UI over native APIs.

design_decisions.decision: ContextPicker and ContextChip are separate components under panels/chat/
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing pattern where chat sub-components (AbortButton, MessageGroup, ThinkingBlock, etc.) are separate files under panels/chat/. Each is independently testable and reusable.

design_decisions.decision: Automatic context (selectedEntity) is shown as a subtle label on user messages, not as a dismissible chip
design_decisions.rationale: The task spec distinguishes between explicit context (chips above input, user-selected) and automatic context (always included, shown as subtle label '[Context: X selected]'). Making it non-dismissible keeps it visually distinct from user-attached context items.

design_decisions.decision: Error classification in a pure utility function separate from the store
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the store focused on state management. The classifier is independently testable and can be reused by other components. The store calls classifyError() and adds the result as a system message.

design_decisions.decision: ChatPanel props extended rather than creating a wrapper component
design_decisions.rationale: The existing ChatPanel is already rendered in App.tsx at the right structural position. Adding props for context data keeps the component tree flat, avoids unnecessary re-renders, and follows the existing pattern where panels receive all needed data as props.

design_decisions.decision: DiffView takes before/after strings and computes diff internally
design_decisions.rationale: Encapsulation: the component owns its rendering logic. ToolCallCard just passes source strings from tool_input and result. DiffView is independently testable and reusable.

design_decisions.decision: StatusBar Claude indicator is optional via props, maintaining backward compatibility
design_decisions.rationale: Adding claudeStatus as an optional prop means existing StatusBar tests continue passing without modification. The Claude section only renders when the prop is provided, so the component works both with and without Claude integration.

reuse.what: claudeStore.sendMessage already accepts context parameter and forwards via onSend callback
reuse.how: Refine the context parameter type from Record<string,unknown> to a structured MessageContext interface. The onSend callback already forwards context to sidecar, so the wiring exists.

reuse.what: Sidecar SendMessage.context already defines selected_entity, diagnostics, constraints fields
reuse.how: The MessageContext type should mirror this structure so context flows directly from UI → store → sidecar without transformation.

reuse.what: ToolCallCard already detects reify_update_source and shows 'View diff' summary text
reuse.how: Extend the existing resultSummary logic and the expanded details section to render DiffView instead of raw JSON when the tool is reify_update_source.

reuse.what: StatusBar section+divider pattern with data-* attributes for CSS styling
reuse.how: Add a new Claude section following the identical pattern: <span class={styles.divider}/> <span class={styles.section}> with label + value, using data-claude-status attribute for state-based styling.

reuse.what: ConstraintPanel.onConstraintSelect callback pattern for optional prop callbacks
reuse.how: Add parallel onAskClaude optional callback following the same pattern. Wire in App.tsx the same way handleConstraintSelect is wired.
Extend inputArea to include context picker button alongside textarea and send/abort. Add contextChips area above inputArea.

reuse.what: Test patterns: factory helpers + data-testid + vi.fn() + render/screen/fireEvent
reuse.how: All new tests follow identical patterns: makeX() factory functions for test data, vi.fn() for callbacks, render(() => <Component />), screen.getByTestId(), fireEvent.click/contextMenu.

reuse.what: CSS modules with var(--reify-*) design tokens
reuse.how: All new CSS modules use the same design tokens (--reify-accent, --reify-background, --reify-text, --reify-surface0, --reify-border, --reify-error, --reify-success) for consistent theming.

reuse.what: Expand/collapse toggle pattern: createSignal(bool) + Show component
reuse.how: DiffView uses the same toggle pattern for collapsible diff content.

reuse.what: useChatPanelPersistence hook for chat panel open/closed state
reuse.how: Reuse for the chat panel toggle triggered by StatusBar click and Ask Claude actions.
